disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Progressive worsening of exertional breathlessness over ~3 weeks on a background of long-standing severe chronic lung disease
trigger_question_examples: How is your breathing compared with usual?
When did the breathlessness start getting worse?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My breathing's been getting worse for about three weeks. I've had bad lungs for years but I'm running out of puff doing almost nothing now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: New bilateral leg swelling extending up to the thighs together with abdominal distension and a tight, bloated belly
trigger_question_examples: Is the swelling in one leg or both?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both my legs have blown up like balloons, right up to the thighs, and my belly feels tight and bloated. This is all new for me.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dull ache in the right upper abdomen (congestive hepatic stretch)
trigger_question_examples: Does the bloating come with any pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a dull ache under my ribs on the right side, nothing sharp, just a heavy full feeling.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever and no new purulent (green/yellow) sputum, and no acute chest pain — pertinent negatives arguing against a primary infective COPD exacerbation or acute coronary event
trigger_question_examples: Any fevers or coughing up discoloured phlegm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fevers, and I'm not coughing up any green or yellow stuff. No real chest pain either — it's the swelling and the breathing.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Has home oxygen prescribed but has been non-compliant / not using it much recently
trigger_question_examples: Do you have home oxygen, and have you been using it?
How often do you use your oxygen machine?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got an oxygen machine at home but I'll be honest, I haven't been using it much lately.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Not currently taking any diuretic / water tablet; only inhaled bronchodilators
trigger_question_examples: Are you on any water tablets / fluid tablets?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Just my puffers for the lungs. No water tablets or anything like that.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Currently alert and able to speak in full sentences; denies confusion or extreme drowsiness (present-state screen that sets the escalation floor below an immediate 000 call)
trigger_question_examples: Are you feeling confused or very drowsy?
Can you speak in full sentences without stopping for breath?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm not confused or falling asleep, and I can still talk in sentences — I just get puffed.
